codeReview.summary: Pass. The original canonicalization commit already removed the raw-token reset fallback and canonicalized company binding, but its recovery proof was blocking because `AuthPasswordResetPublicContractIT` manually inserted a digest row after calling forgot-password. The fix commit resolves that gap: `canonicalRecoveryFlow_forgotResetLoginRefreshAndMe_succeeds_withDigestBackedResetToken()` now captures the real forgot-issued token from `EmailService` (`AuthPasswordResetPublicContractIT.java:301-309`), proves the forgot flow persisted it as digest-only with `token_digest` set and `token` null (`AuthPasswordResetPublicContractIT.java:311-324`), and then drives reset -> login -> refresh -> `/auth/me` with canonical `companyCode` / `X-Company-Code`. Combined with the class's explicit legacy raw-row rejection test (`AuthPasswordResetPublicContractIT.java:274-287`), digest-only reset lookup in `PasswordResetService` (`PasswordResetService.java:162`), and the existing canonical company-context assertions in `AuthControllerIT` (`AuthControllerIT.java:92,111`) plus `CompanyContextFilter` (`CompanyContextFilter.java:114,130-139`), the prior VAL-CROSS-001 blocking gap is resolved and the related canonical auth assertions remain covered.

summary: PASS. The previous blocking review is resolved: commit 5a126de5 replaces the manual reset-token row insertion with real forgot-issued token capture and digest-only persistence assertions, so the canonical forgot -> reset -> login -> refresh -> /auth/me proof now exercises the intended production path end-to-end. No new blocking or non-blocking code defects were found in the fix.
